topic: Validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop: structural layout, bidirectional path-coupling repair, the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow, reference migration across commands/agents/READMEs/advisor-corpus, and whether fallback-router.ts should be wired for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback.

normalized_topic: validate and stress-test the merge design for folding deep-loop-runtime into deep-loop-workflows as system-deep-loop
note: Spec writeback was intentionally not performed because the detached lineage prompt forbids writes outside the lineage artifact directory.

focus: Structural merge layout and directional path coupling
noveltyJustification: First evidence pass established the actual target layout and confirmed both path-coupling directions directly in code.
keyQuestions: Is the proposed system-deep-loop target layout structurally sound?
Are the forward and reverse path-coupling repair rules correct?
answeredQuestions: Is the proposed system-deep-loop target layout structurally sound?
Are the forward and reverse path-coupling repair rules correct?

keyFindings.title: Target layout is coherent if runtime stays infrastructure-only
keyFindings.summary: The proposed layout keeps four workflow packets at hub level and nests former runtime under runtime/ with no mode-registry entry, matching the parent spec's out-of-scope rule against behavior changes.

keyFindings.title: Forward coupling rule is correct
keyFindings.summary: Runtime-to-workflows imports such as render-command-contract.cjs currently climb to a sibling deep-loop-workflows folder; after both trees move under system-deep-loop/runtime and system-deep-loop/shared, the hop count is unchanged and the deep-loop-workflows segment is deleted.

keyFindings.title: Reverse coupling rule is correct
keyFindings.summary: Workflow packets importing runtime, such as deep-research reduce-state and ai-council orchestrate-topic, currently climb to sibling deep-loop-runtime; after nesting, runtime is one level closer under the hub, so the required repair is minus one hop plus runtime/.

keyFindings.title: One graph-metadata file is necessary
keyFindings.summary: Current separate graph metadata has reciprocal deep-loop-workflows/deep-loop-runtime edges; the unified skill should drop those structural edges and fresh-author one system-deep-loop graph metadata.

keyFindings.title: Stage-0 quiesce remains load-bearing
keyFindings.summary: The target move includes runtime/database and observability artifacts, so preserving line counts/checksums and confirming no in-flight loop remains a real precondition, not paperwork.

ruledOut.reason: The target layout explicitly treats runtime as infrastructure, not an invokable mode.

focus: system-spec-kit tooling-borrow and command contract path repair
noveltyJustification: Second pass confirmed the tooling-borrow spans both runtime and system-spec-kit and cannot be deferred to external prose migration.
keyQuestions: Does the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow need same-phase repair?
Are compiled command contracts/codegen part of the migration risk?
answeredQuestions: Does the system-spec-kit tooling-borrow need same-phase repair?
Are compiled command contracts/codegen part of the migration risk?

keyFindings.title: Tooling-borrow repair belongs in structural phase 002
keyFindings.summary: runtime/package.json and runtime/tsconfig.json borrow system-spec-kit TypeScript tooling, while system-spec-kit test:council and vitest include globs point back into deep-loop-runtime tests; all four paths become wrong at move time.

keyFindings.title: artifact-root.cjs is another system-spec-kit dependency seam
keyFindings.summary: The runtime artifact-root wrapper reaches into system-spec-kit/shared/review-research-paths.cjs, so the move should verify this seam alongside package.json and tsconfig rather than assuming only test tooling changes.

keyFindings.title: Compiled command contracts must be regenerated, not hand-edited
keyFindings.summary: /deep:* command frontmatter invokes render-command-contract.cjs, whose sources and drift checker enumerate old deep-loop paths; migration must update sources and regenerate compiled contracts to keep hashes authoritative.

keyFindings.title: compile-command-contracts has an anchor-depth hazard
keyFindings.summary: The child plan already notes WORKSPACE_ROOT depth repair; this is correct because nesting runtime one level deeper changes __dirname-relative workspace anchoring even when string literals are also renamed.

ruledOut.approach: Defer system-spec-kit test path updates to child 003
ruledOut.reason: The runtime move would silently remove system-spec-kit council test coverage if the globs are missed; this is structural, not external prose migration.

focus: External reference migration and advisor corpus risk
noveltyJustification: Third pass verified the broad reference surface and found plugin fail-open behavior that increases the risk of a missed path.
keyQuestions: Is the reference migration stage complete enough across commands, agents, docs, plugins, and advisor corpus?
answeredQuestions: Is the reference migration stage complete enough across commands, agents, docs, plugins, and advisor corpus?

keyFindings.title: External reference migration is genuinely broad
keyFindings.summary: A live scoped rg count found 764 old-name hits across 77 files in commands, agents, advisor, plugin, GitHub/root README surfaces, consistent with the phase plan's claim that this is not a simple rename.

keyFindings.title: Advisor has duplicated hardcoded merged-skill identity
keyFindings.summary: Python skill_advisor.py and TypeScript aliases.ts both hardcode deep-loop-workflows as MERGED_DEEP_SKILL_ID, while explicit.ts boosts /deep phrases to the same identity; these must update as a reviewed pair.

keyFindings.title: Advisor corpus requires field-scoped edits and rebaseline
keyFindings.summary: labeled-prompts.jsonl stores deep-loop-workflows in skill_top_1 fields for deep-loop prompts; changing label fields without altering prompt text preserves historical corpus meaning and supports accuracy comparison.

keyFindings.title: mk-deep-loop-guard missing-path behavior is silent
keyFindings.summary: The guard plugin loads .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json and returns null on any read/parse error; the hook then skips mismatch checks when registry is null, and documented behavior is fail-open.

keyFindings.title: Freezing /deep:* commands and agent names is still the right scope
keyFindings.summary: The problem is two skill identities for one family, not public command names. Renaming /deep:* or agent names would multiply the migration surface without resolving the identity split.

ruledOut.approach: Blind repo-wide find/replace including prompt text and specs history
ruledOut.reason: Specs history is explicitly non-breaking history, and advisor prompt/corpus fields need different safety rules.

focus: Fallback-router wiring decision and convergence check
noveltyJustification: Fourth pass mostly confirmed prior design; the only additive insight is to defer fallback wiring unless this research phase must remediate failed GLM replicas before merge execution.
keyQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback?
Has the research reached legal convergence?
answeredQuestions: Should fallback-router.ts be wired now for real GLM-5.2 to MiMo-v2.5-Pro fallback?
Has the research reached legal convergence?

keyFindings.title: fallback-router.ts is ready but unused by fan-out
keyFindings.summary: The router validates known targets, scope, cycles, and hop count and resolveFallback returns fallback or fail-fast routes, but live non-test references are only the module definitions; fanout-pool.cjs still terminates after same-model retry exhaustion.

keyFindings.title: Fallback wiring should remain optional for the structural merge
keyFindings.summary: The parent spec explicitly scopes workflow behavior changes out of the merge and tracks fallback wiring as optional; wiring it into fanout-pool is real behavior work and should not block rename/nesting.

keyFindings.title: Wire fallback before re-running GLM-heavy research only if operator wants automatic remediation
keyFindings.summary: If failed GLM replicas must be auto-substituted before this research fanout is repeated, phase 004 should land first; otherwise manual MiMo redispatch remains acceptable because fanout-merge enumerates lineage directories.

keyFindings.title: Legal convergence reached after 4 iterations
keyFindings.summary: All five key questions have evidence-backed answers, source diversity includes specs, workflow YAML, runtime code, advisor code, plugin code, and package/test config, and the latest pass produced low novelty.

ruledOut.approach: Treat fallback-router wiring as required inside phase 002 or 003
ruledOut.reason: It changes fan-out behavior and retry semantics, while 002/003 are structural/reference migrations.
